SOFR eased to 3.66% vs IORB 3.65% — spread narrowing but still above

credit.label: Improving

ALLY +0.48%, CACC +1.23%, COF +1.47% — lenders extending gains
No credit stress despite Korea/semis rout deepening further

vol.label: Global vol calming, Korea-domestic crisis deepening
decomp: MOVE flat, no new print — bond vol still calm
Gold +2.40% — safe-haven bid extending a 2nd session
USDJPY fell back below 162.00 BOJ threshold
KOSPI -7.89% to a fresh post-selloff low of 7,648.10

bias: The Korea/semis rout is bifurcating rather than resolving: the Korean domestic epicenter keeps deepening — KOSPI -7.89% to a fresh post-selloff low of 7,648.10, SK Hynix -14.57%, Samsung -9.06% — while the US-listed read-through is stabilizing or reversing: MU +1.04%, ASX +3.35%, SOXX back to roughly flat (-0.42%) after yesterday's -6.41%. EWY itself is flat (+0.15%) despite the KOSPI plunge. The Korea gate stays 0/3 — EWY below its $197.32 SMA20, USDKRW at 1,542.98 (still above the 1,530 BOK watch level), KOSPI well below 8,200. Elsewhere, risk signals continue to calm: VIX -3.68% to 15.97, MOVE flat, financials (ALLY/CACC/COF) all firm and extending gains, and USDJPY has fallen back below the 162.00 BOJ threshold (-0.97%). Gold is up another +2.40%, extending its safe-haven bid for a second session — the one signal arguing this episode has more than one day's legs. Net: this looks increasingly like a contained, Korea-domestic dislocation (the chip majors and the local index, not the broader chain or US macro) rather than a systemic liquidity or rate event, but the persistent gold bid and the still-falling KOSPI argue for keeping the Korea gate closed and the EWY/Korea complex avoided until the local index actually stabilizes.

counterargs.thesis: Korea/semis rout is a specific, Korea-domestic catalyst, not a macro regime shift
supporting: VIX -3.68% to 15.97 — global vol compressing, not spiking
MOVE flat, still calm — no rates contagion
ALLY, CACC, COF and HYG all up today — credit and lenders unfazed
US-listed chip proxies (MU +1.04%, ASX +3.35%, SOXX ~flat) stabilizing even as Korea falls further
against: KOSPI -7.89% to a fresh post-selloff low of 7,648.10
SK Hynix -14.57%, Samsung -9.06% — the actual Korean chip majors accelerating lower, not stabilizing
Korea gate still 0/3, unchanged from this morning
Gold +2.40% for a second straight session — a genuine, persistent safe-haven bid
counterargs.read: The divergence has sharpened rather than resolved: US-listed proxies and broad macro vol both argue this is contained, but the Korean domestic names are getting worse, not better — the opposite of what you'd expect from a one-off technical air pocket. Until KOSPI, SK Hynix, and Samsung show a real stabilization, keep the Korea complex fully avoided regardless of how calm SOXX/MU look.

trade_table.rationale: US-listed proxies (MU +1.04%, ASX +3.35%, SOXX ~flat) are stabilizing after two rough sessions, but the Korean chip majors (SK Hynix -14.57%, Samsung -9.06%) are still falling — too early to re-engage.
10Y essentially flat at 4.477%. No fresh signal either way; watch the delayed H.4.1 print for the next catalyst.
Gate remains 0/3. KOSPI fell to a fresh low (7,648.10, -7.89%) even as EWY itself is flat — until the local index stabilizes, stay out regardless of gate math.
ALLY +0.48%, CACC +1.23%, COF +1.47% — extending the divergence for a third straight session. HY OAS steady at 2.75%.
USDJPY back below the 162.00 BOJ threshold (-0.97%), DXY -0.63%. First real give-back in dollar strength this episode.
HYG +0.21%, OAS flat at 2.75% — credit still not confirming any equity stress, now into a third session.
Jul–Sep reserve drain still ahead, and this week remains a live demonstration of how fast sector-specific shocks can compound for levered names.

catalysts.note: TGA + Reserves print due today; still showing Jun 24 data as of this run. Will show if the drain rate is accelerating.
Strong print = Warsh hike back on table. Weak print = duration relief.
Bid-to-cover and tail will show whether private demand absorbs supply at these higher yields.
PCE peak narrative confirmed or reversed. Hot CPI = duration selloff + Warsh hike.
APH $165/$170, DRAM $69/$74, EOSE $7.50/$10 spreads all expire — Korea/semis volatility has materially worsened the P&L picture here.
Quarterly Refunding Announcement. Auction sizes + TGA guidance for Q3.

oil.note: Oil extends its decline, well off the war-era highs. No inflation impulse; supportive of the PCE-peak narrative.
